evidence: Error handling conventions diverge across sibling integration layers: `src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/langchain_tools.py` catches broad `Exception` and returns error strings from wrappers, while `examples/a2a_gateway/demo_orchestrator/remote_agent.py` raises `RuntimeError` and `docker/shared/config_loader.py` raises `ValueError` for operational failures.

Package surface conventions are inconsistent: `src/thenvoi/__init__.py` and `src/thenvoi/runtime/__init__.py` expose very large re-export APIs, while sibling integration packages like `src/thenvoi/integrations/anthropic/__init__.py`, `src/thenvoi/integrations/parlant/__init__.py`, and `src/thenvoi/integrations/pydantic_ai/__init__.py` export nothing (`__all__ = []`).

Example module conventions drift: `examples/a2a_gateway/demo_orchestrator/__init__.py` uses broad `try/except ImportError` fallback import style, while sibling example packages use simple static exports/docstring-only init files.

Tool binding is manually reimplemented in multiple adapters: `PydanticAIAdapter._create_agent()` registers many near-identical wrapper functions (`src/thenvoi/adapters/pydantic_ai.py`), `ClaudeSDKAdapter._create_mcp_server()` defines many near-identical MCP tool handlers (`src/thenvoi/adapters/claude_sdk.py`), and `agent_tools_to_langchain()` repeats the same pattern (`src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/langchain_tools.py`).

The codebase has a centralized tool catalog (`TOOL_MODELS`, `ALL_TOOL_NAMES`) in `src/thenvoi/runtime/tools.py`, but adapter internals still duplicate low-level dispatch and error-string behavior by hand.

Peer resolution logic is duplicated between adapter/server layers (`src/thenvoi/integrations/a2a/gateway/adapter.py::_resolve_peer` and `src/thenvoi/integrations/a2a/gateway/server.py::_resolve_peer`), and server UUID fallback performs a per-request linear scan over all peers to recover slug.

Contact-management methods are duplicated almost verbatim between src/thenvoi/runtime/tools.py (list_contacts/add_contact/remove_contact/list_contact_requests/respond_contact_request at lines ~731-952) and src/thenvoi/runtime/contact_tools.py (same method set at lines ~55-276).

Docker runner lifecycle logic is repeated across docker/claude_sdk/runner.py, docker/codex/runner.py, and examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py: same shutdown-event pattern, env validation, mount validation, retry loop, and agent-run orchestration.

Character/example agents repeat the same bootstrapping flow (sys.path mutation, load_dotenv, THENVOI_WS_URL/THENVOI_REST_URL validation, load_agent_config, Agent.create) across multiple files such as examples/anthropic/03_tom_agent.py, examples/claude_sdk/03_tom_agent.py, examples/pydantic_ai/03_tom_agent.py, and examples/langgraph/08_jerry_agent.py.

thenvoi-bridge/bridge_core/bridge.py is 721 LOC and combines config parsing, reconnect policy, signal/shutdown handling, room participant caching, dedupe, and message dispatch orchestration in one class.

Nine example logging modules repeat the same setup skeleton with tiny parameter differences (e.g., examples/a2a_bridge/setup_logging.py, examples/langgraph/setup_logging.py, examples/pydantic_ai/setup_logging.py, examples/claude_sdk/setup_logging.py).

Tool adapter layers replicate near-identical wrapper/catch/return-string patterns across frameworks instead of using one shared wrapper builder (src/thenvoi/adapters/pydantic_ai.py, src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/langchain_tools.py, src/thenvoi/adapters/claude_sdk.py, src/thenvoi/integrations/parlant/tools.py).

Several modules contain tutorial-style, highly repetitive doc/prompt blocks far larger than the executable logic they describe (examples/langgraph/prompts.py, examples/prompts/characters.py, src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/graph_tools.py, src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/message_formatters.py).

findings.summary: Sibling adapter/tool layers use conflicting error contracts (string-return, typed exception, and wrapped RuntimeError), creating behavioral outliers.
evidence: `agent_tools_to_langchain()` wrappers in `langchain_tools.py` catch broad `Exception` and return string payloads like `"Error sending message: ..."` instead of propagating exceptions.
`create_parlant_tools()` in `parlant/tools.py` similarly catches broad exceptions and returns `ToolResult(data="Error: ...")` for operational failures.
`GatewayClient.call_peer()` in `remote_agent.py` catches generic exceptions, logs, and raises `RuntimeError`, while `config_loader.py` raises `ValueError` for YAML/file I/O failures.
findings.suggestion: Define one cross-integration error policy for tool boundaries (for example: raise typed domain exceptions internally and normalize only at outer transport boundary), then refactor wrapper/tool modules to follow it consistently.

findings.summary: Broad `ImportError` fallback import pattern in demo orchestrator is a style island that can hide genuine dependency/import failures.
evidence: `examples/a2a_gateway/demo_orchestrator/__init__.py` wraps relative imports in `try/except ImportError` and falls back to bare imports (`from agent import ...`).

Sibling modules (`agent.py`, `agent_executor.py`) use bare intra-directory imports (`from remote_agent import GatewayClient`, `from agent import OrchestratorAgent`) rather than a single consistent package-relative convention.
Catching `ImportError` broadly here can also catch transitive import errors from dependencies, not only package-vs-script import mode.
findings.suggestion: Use one explicit import convention for this package (prefer package-relative imports) and avoid broad fallback except clauses; if dual-mode execution is required, gate it with explicit `__package__` checks or launcher entrypoints.

findings.summary: Public API exposure strategy in `__init__.py` files is inconsistent, with oversized re-export hubs alongside empty integration package surfaces.
evidence: `src/thenvoi/__init__.py` and `src/thenvoi/runtime/__init__.py` expose large, multi-concern symbol lists via `__all__`.
Integration sibling packages use diverging export behavior: some export internal utilities (`integrations/claude_sdk/__init__.py`), others force empty API (`__all__ = []`).
This creates non-uniform discoverability/import patterns across adjacent modules in the same subsystem.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a package-surface convention document (thin stable facades vs internal-only packages), then normalize `__init__.py` exports across integration and runtime packages to match that convention.

findings.summary: Example logging setup modules duplicate the same helper wrapper but diverge in options and naming/documentation conventions without a shared policy.
evidence: All modules define similarly named `setup_logging` wrappers around `setup_example_logging`, but parameterization differs ad hoc (`root_level`, `warning_loggers`, `stream_stdout`, custom formats, `extra_level_loggers`).
Docstring intent is inconsistent for nearly identical wrappers (some detailed rationale, others minimal), making sibling behavior harder to predict.
No shared convention layer exists for per-framework logging profiles despite repeating the same structure.
findings.suggestion: Centralize example logging profiles (e.g., per-framework config map) and keep each example setup module as a thin declarative profile selector to remove drift and enforce consistent conventions.

findings.summary: Three adapter paths hand-code the same tool-wrapper pattern instead of sharing one binding mechanism.
evidence: `pydantic_ai.py` registers many `agent.tool(...)` wrappers that mostly do `await ctx.deps.<tool>(...)` with repeated try/except stringification.
`claude_sdk.py` defines many `@tool(...)` handlers with repeated room lookup, `No tools available` checks, and `_make_error(...)` boilerplate.
`langchain_tools.py` defines many async wrapper functions with the same exception-to-string pattern before `StructuredTool.from_function(...)`.
A canonical tool registry already exists in `runtime/tools.py` (`TOOL_MODELS`, `ALL_TOOL_NAMES`), but adapter low-level bindings are still copy/pasted per framework.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single internal tool-binding factory (e.g., declarative table: tool name -> callable signature/adapter codec) that generates framework-specific wrappers for PydanticAI, Claude SDK MCP, and LangChain. Keep framework-specific marshaling only at the final boundary and centralize error mapping once.

findings.summary: A wide `AgentToolsProtocol` forces broad mocking and wrapper churn, reducing interface honesty.
evidence: `AgentToolsProtocol` aggregates chat, participant, contact, and memory APIs into one large interface in `core/protocols.py`.
Adapter tests repeatedly construct partial tool mocks (`MagicMock` + many `AsyncMock` assignments) for each framework test file rather than using narrow capability interfaces.
`SimpleAdapter.on_event()` passes a single `inp.tools` object into every adapter path, encouraging adapters to depend on the same broad protocol regardless of actual needs.
findings.suggestion: Split `AgentToolsProtocol` into capability-focused protocols (e.g., MessagingTools, ParticipantTools, ContactTools, MemoryTools) and type adapters against the smallest required protocol. Provide a composed concrete `AgentTools` that implements all capabilities, while tests can mock only the needed subset.

findings.summary: Gateway peer resolution is duplicated across layers and server fallback does avoidable O(n) scanning.
evidence: Both adapter and server implement separate `_resolve_peer()` logic for slug/UUID fallback.
`GatewayServer._resolve_peer()` performs `for slug, p in self.peers.items()` when UUID is used, scanning all peers per call.
Behavioral assumptions about slug-vs-UUID resolution are tested at adapter level, but resolution mechanics are split between two implementations, increasing drift risk.
findings.suggestion: Create one shared resolver component that stores both `slug->peer` and `uuid->slug` maps and is used by both adapter and server. Remove linear scan fallback and return `(slug, peer)` in O(1) for both key types.

findings.summary: Contact operations are implemented twice with near-identical flow, splitting one responsibility across two classes.
evidence: Both classes define the same five contact methods with matching parameter shapes and response shaping logic (list/add/remove/list_requests/respond).
Both implementations build dynamic kwargs for nullable identifiers and duplicate metadata mapping logic for received/sent contact requests.
This creates two behavior sources for the same API contract, increasing drift risk when contact payloads evolve.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared `ContactService` (or mixin/composition helper) that owns all contact API calls and response mapping, then have `AgentTools` and `ContactTools` delegate to it with only context-specific wrapping.

findings.summary: Contact-state cleanup workflow is copy-pasted across integration suites instead of centralized.
evidence: Each file defines its own `cleanup_contact_state(api_client, api_client_2)` with the same multi-step sequence: remove contacts, cancel outgoing requests, reject incoming pending requests, sleep for state settling.
The duplicated helpers are repeatedly invoked throughout each suite, multiplying maintenance cost for any contact-state policy change.
findings.suggestion: Move cleanup orchestration into a shared fixture/helper module (for example tests/integration/contact_state.py or conftest fixture) and parameterize the small behavioral differences, so all suites consume one canonical cleanup contract.

findings.summary: Example agents repeatedly embed the same startup choreography instead of using a shared bootstrap layer.
evidence: Across these scripts, `main()` repeats identical setup steps: `load_dotenv()`, env URL retrieval/validation, credentials loading, adapter init, `Agent.create(...)`, and `agent.run()`.
Multiple scripts also mutate import paths with `sys.path.insert(...)` to reach shared prompt modules, indicating shared example infrastructure is missing.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared example bootstrap utility (e.g., `examples/common/bootstrap.py`) that returns normalized runtime config and agent factory inputs; keep each example focused on adapter-specific deltas and scenario behavior.

findings.summary: Runner entrypoints duplicate orchestration logic, coupling operational policy to each adapter script.
evidence: All runner files define the same structural components: module-level shutdown event, signal handlers, AGENT_CONFIG and THENVOI URL validation, mount validation, retry counters, and `while not _shutdown_event.is_set()` loops.
Behavioral policy (retry semantics, shutdown choreography, env precedence) is encoded in each runner, so reliability fixes must be applied in several places.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared runner core that owns lifecycle orchestration (signals, retries, shutdown, env checks), and keep adapter-specific runners as thin config/build hooks.

findings.summary: ThenvoiBridge concentrates too many responsibilities, making behavioral changes high-risk.
evidence: thenvoi-bridge/bridge_core/bridge.py is 721 LOC and includes env/config parsing (`BridgeConfig.from_env`), reconnect strategy, signal/shutdown control, room discovery, dedup cache handling, participant caching, and message routing integration.
The class combines lifecycle management and message-domain behavior, forcing unrelated changes (e.g., reconnection tuning vs routing logic) through one large control surface.
findings.suggestion: Split `ThenvoiBridge` into focused components (configuration loader, connection supervisor, room state/cache manager, dispatch coordinator) and keep bridge.py as composition/root wiring only.

findings.summary: Example logging setup is copy-pasted across many files with only minor option changes.
evidence: Each file defines a same-named `setup_logging(...)` wrapper around `setup_example_logging(...)` with mostly duplicated imports/docstrings and only small argument differences (extra/warning/debug logger tuples).

Two pairs are byte-identical by hash (`examples/langgraph/setup_logging.py` and `examples/pydantic_ai/setup_logging.py`; `examples/a2a_bridge/setup_logging.py` and duplicate entry in hash scan), indicating template cloning rather than intentional module-specific logic.
findings.suggestion: Replace per-example copies with one shared `examples/common_logging.py` helper that accepts a profile/options object, and keep only thin one-line config declarations in each example package.

findings.summary: Multiple adapter modules duplicate generated-style tool wrappers with identical broad-except/error-string handling.
evidence: In `src/thenvoi/adapters/pydantic_ai.py`, many `thenvoi_*` functions repeat `try: return await ctx.deps.<tool>(...) except Exception as e: return f"Error ..."` (e.g., lines around 125-406).

In `src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/langchain_tools.py`, wrappers repeat the same `except Exception as e` pattern for each tool (e.g., lines 38-214) plus repeated `StructuredTool.from_function(...)` boilerplate (lines 218+).
In `src/thenvoi/adapters/claude_sdk.py` and `src/thenvoi/integrations/parlant/tools.py`, the same per-tool pattern appears again with broad catch blocks and near-duplicate error shaping.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared declarative tool registry (tool name, callable, argument schema, error policy) plus one generic wrapper factory to generate framework-specific bindings; centralize exception mapping once instead of per-tool/per-adapter duplication.

findings.summary: Prompt/doc text is excessively repetitive and dominates logic in several runtime-facing modules.
evidence: `examples/langgraph/prompts.py` repeats the same mandatory-send-message rule in multiple sections (`CRITICAL`, `MANDATORY`, `FINAL REMINDER`) instead of one canonical constraint block.
`examples/prompts/characters.py` embeds long repeated instruction sections (multiple `CRITICAL` blocks, long examples and rule restatements) that are hard to maintain and prone to divergence.
`src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/graph_tools.py` and `message_formatters.py` include tutorial-level walkthrough prose and extended examples that restate behavior visible in short function bodies.
findings.suggestion: Split long instructional content into reusable prompt fragments/templates with a single source of truth; trim module docstrings to concise API contracts and move tutorials/examples to dedicated docs files.
